body.title: Day 2026-07-09
body.text:
## Done
13 tickets closed, 12 proofs emitted, all pushed, tree clean.

## The one thing
On 2026-06-26 the goal->intention switch deleted the `links.goals` EDGE (memory_emit.LINK_KINDS,
ticket_store._LINK_KEYS) and never added a successor. Intentions kept being captured -- 444 times,
as free-text prose stapled to a ticket, plus 215 nulls -- in a shape nothing could point at. The
same hole let the aider gate close a ticket `PASS` on three 0-byte files: its ticket carried
`intention: null`, so the gate had nothing to check the build against. Edge restored, PROVEN
(T-links-intentions-edge-kind, red=AssertionError -> green=passed, bcb28a75).

## Measured (conditions recorded)
- Capability is a SET, not a ladder: deepseek-r1:14b passes b5-frobenius; the 32b fails it at
  4096/8192/32768 with a clean `stop`. `difficulty_meets`'s `>=` filter is unsound.
- `frontier` has no measured holder (T-frontier-has-no-measured-holder, status=akien).
- The eval corpus does not discriminate: b4-boxes leaks its answer token twice in its own prompt.
- validity_sweep checks 7 store entries; 0 of 130 proofs carry validity_conditions.
- shipped-unproven: 49 closes, 17 named a proof method, 1 ever revisited; 8 of the 17 name the
  same one -- "no red form".

## Intention Based Development (design, with Akien)
An intention states how the system IS, present tense, even unbuilt -- it is the CONTRACT, and a
conflicting contract reconciles TO it before ticketing. Intention + why lets an LLM sort intent
always; a why-less line is a contract hole that cannot arbitrate. 90% is a stipulated ceiling, not
a statistic: enum (provisional|proved), never a float. Proving is RETROACTIVE both ways -- a claim
rises when its proof method arrives, and falls back when its recorded conditions move -- so
shipped-unproven is not terminal. Red is made of git history and evaporates once the impl is not
HEAD~1; mutation-red is the general proof method, and it also detects the echo-test. To mutate you
must know what to break: the property IS the intention. "Lever" renamed to "proof method" after the
metaphor of force made CC write a false line (the proof CORPUS earns a gate's removal, never the
backlog). Membership test: an intention belongs in the layer only if it cannot be derived from a
standing intention plus its why.

## Artifacts
akien/inbox/20260709.CCReviewOfIntentionsOutline.txt is the markup master (43 contract holes,
7 conflicts / 4 resolved, 19 [CC] lines to ratify). Plus IntentionsHarvest.txt (121 of Akien's
typed statements from 207 transcripts) and IntentionsByDevice.txt (565 store-held, 34 devices).

## Audit
3 known failures (2 deliberate guard reds + 1 pre-existing arch-point TypeError), all ticketed.
Full suite NOT run: tests/igor has 9 collection errors that abort it, and the rest exceeds 10 min
(standing rule: overnight). Stated, not papered. Stashes 0. Path-moves clean. Consequence 0 overdue.
